repository.description: Automate changelogs, versioning, and publishing—even for monorepos across multiple package registries 🧭

commit.message:
Feat/emacs pkg (#4)

* feat: add summary DTOs and QuerySummaries for lightweight log listing

- Create internal/management/dto.go with LogSummary, LogListResponse,
  LogDetailResponse, LogDetailEntry, StreamViewResponse, BodyResponse,
  and APIError types
- Add database.LogSummary struct to models.go for DB scan targets
- Add LogReader.QuerySummaries() method selecting only summary columns
  (no bodies/headers) with computed body-length fields
- Update apiLogsHandler to use QuerySummaries and return LogListResponse
  envelope with pagination metadata (limit, offset, has_more)
- Update apiLogByIDHandler to return LogDetailResponse with decoded
  headers and no body fields
- Add writeAPIError, decodeHeaders, derefStr helpers
- Add ManagementConfig.PreviewBytes (default 65536) with validation
- Update example-config.yaml with preview_bytes setting
- Remove old logDetailResponse/streamViewResponse types from api.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FTS5 full-text search indexing for openai_logs

- Add ftsSchema constant with FTS5 virtual table and sync triggers
- Add migrateFTS() with automatic rebuild for existing data
- Update buildWhere() to use FTS5 MATCH instead of LIKE
- Add FTS5 search test with distinct body content
- Enable sqlite_fts5 build tag in build script and Dockerfile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wire query parser into list API and add body retrieval endpoint

- Add query DSL support to apiLogsHandler via the 'query' parameter,
  using internal/query.Parse and query.ToSQL. Old individual params
  (status, path, since, until, q) remain as backward-compatible fallback.
- Add QuerySummariesRaw and CountRaw methods to LogReader that accept
  extra WHERE clause and args for the parsed query DSL.
- Add GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint with part (req/resp), mode
  (raw/assembled), and full parameters. Uses streamview.Build for
  assembled mode.
- Refactor apiLogByIDHandler into apiLogSubHandler that dispatches
  between detail and body sub-routes.
- Add PreviewBytes field to ServerDeps, wired from config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update web UI for new management API endpoint split

- Replace three separate filter inputs (status, path, search) with a
  unified query DSL input that sends a 'query' parameter
- Update detail modal to use new API shape: headers are now parsed
  objects (req_headers/resp_headers) instead of JSON strings
- Add on-demand body loading via GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint
  with Load Request/Response Body buttons and Load Full for truncated
- Stream view assembled toggle now fetches from body endpoint
- Show included_bytes / total_bytes info from body responses
- Update index.html to match new single query filter input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive tests for body endpoint, query DSL, and summary shape

- Body endpoint tests (10): raw req/resp, assembled SSE, invalid
  combination, preview truncation, full mode, not found, empty body,
  default params, invalid part
- Query DSL wiring tests (2): filter by status code, parse error
- Summary list shape test (1): verify has_request_body/has_response_body
  present and body/header fields absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: emacs pkg: memoryelaine (and adptions)

  Implementation Complete — All 21 Tasks Done

  Phase 1: Go Server (11 tasks)

   - API redesign: Summary/detail/body endpoint split — list returns lightweight summaries, detail returns metadata+headers (no bodies), body endpoint serves content on demand
   - FTS5 indexing: Full-text search on request/response bodies with sync triggers and auto-migration
   - Query DSL parser: status:200 method:POST path:/chat "exact phrase" with parameterized SQL (no injection)
   - Web UI + TUI: Updated to use new API (query DSL input, on-demand body loading)
   - 28 server tests (14 new), updated docs and design spec

  Phase 2: Emacs Package (10 tasks)

  8 modules in /work/emacs/:

   - memoryelaine.el — entry point, defcustoms, autoloads
   - memoryelaine-auth.el — auth-source → explicit → interactive prompt
   - memoryelaine-http.el — async curl with generation counters, stale response suppression
   - memoryelaine-state.el — global search + buffer-local detail state
   - memoryelaine-search.el — tabulated-list-mode with query DSL, pagination, recording toggle, live search
   - memoryelaine-show.el — detail buffer with on-demand body loading, stream view toggle
   - memoryelaine-log.el — timestamped diagnostic buffer
   - memoryelaine-test.el — 25 ERT tests, all passing

  Test Results

   - 143 Go tests passing | 25 Emacs ERT tests passing

* Emacs build script, code review, and second opinion

* docs: fix mismatches between spec/README and Go implementation

- Fix DSL flag names: is:truncated -> is:req-truncated/is:resp-truncated,
  has:req-body/has:resp-body -> has:req/has:resp (matches parser.go)
- Fix assembled mode description: returns available:false with reason,
  does not fall back to raw (matches api.go)
- Add limit, offset, has_more to /api/logs response example (matches dto.go)
- Add Emacs client to list of consumer surfaces

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Opus-4.6: Address review from Gemini 3.1 Pro and gpt-5.4

* Add Gemini 3.1 Pro's patch review.

* Add Qwen's review

* gpt-5.4 final review

* Update spec, reorder design docs

* Also run emacs tests in CI

* Fix linting

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: chart list export shows JSON dialog instead of PPTX/SVG/PNG

The export button on the active chart item in the sidebar was showing
the PPTX/SVG/PNG format picker (showExportDialog) instead of a JSON
data export dialog with version selection.

Create a dedicated showChartExportDialog() that shows only version
selection and always exports as .arbol.json. The existing export dialog
remains unchanged for the footer toolbar visual export.

repository.name: arbol
repository.description: Browser-based org chart editor with real-time D3 visualization

commit.message:
fix: load correct tree after deleting active chart

When deleting the active chart, handleDeleteChart() was not calling
onChartSwitch() with the new active chart. This left OrgStore holding
the deleted chart's tree, so the remaining chart displayed stale data.

Now checks if the deleted chart was active, and after deletion calls
onChartSwitch() with the new active chart to update OrgStore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve level and dottedLine in normalizeTreeText()

normalizeTreeText() manually constructs OrgNode objects but was not
copying the level or dottedLine fields. This caused levels mapped
during CSV import to be silently dropped when text normalization
(Title Case, Uppercase, Lowercase) was applied.

Added explicit copying for both fields, matching the pattern used
by filterVisibleTree() and cloneBase() in tree.ts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add download-index command for pre-built index from GitHub Releases

Users can now get semantic search in ~30 seconds instead of 15-30 minutes:
  npx @jasonbellz/gospel-library-mcp download-index

Downloads index.db from the latest GitHub Release and saves it to
~/.gospel-library-mcp/index.db. Uses atomic write (.tmp + rename)
to avoid corruption if interrupted.

Also:
- Export DB_DIR from vectorStore.ts (needed by downloader)
- Update startup/search fallback messages to suggest download-index first
- Update build-index message to reflect title+description indexing

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> erstelle einen Bereich mit Tipps und Tricks zur Erreichung des
verbliebenen Rest


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

⚡ Quickly spin up Copilot coding agent tasks from anywhere on your macOS
or Windows machine with [Raycast](https://gh.io/cca-raycast-docs).

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat(checkin): Add state persistence and restore

Implement RestoreEntity persistence via custom
CheckinExtraStoredData class with ISO 8601 datetime
serialization. Add async_added_to_hass with state
restore and stale state validation that auto-corrects
checked_in/awaiting states when event boundaries have
passed during restart. Add 15 unit tests covering
restoration, stale validation, and ExtraStoredData.

Phase 4 of check-in/check-out tracking feature
(tasks.md: T016-T020).

commit.message:
fix: graceful 404 handling and improved category guidance

- browse_category: return helpful guidance on 404 instead of crashing
  with 'isError: true'. Includes correct Come Follow Me path examples.
- index.ts: browse_category handler shows suggestion text as a normal
  response (not an error) when notFound=true
- browse_category tool description: warn that paths are year-specific,
  instruct Copilot to use search_gospel_library first when unsure
- search.ts: add come-follow-me, cfm, primary, seminary category aliases
- indexer.ts: add Come Follow Me (individuals+families, sunday school)
  to indexed categories for semantic search

commit.message:
Fix(checkin): Use dynamic follow-on event lookup

Replace hardcoded _get_next_event() (index 1) in
_compute_linger_timing with _find_followon_event()
that scans coordinator data for the first event not
matching the checked-out event key and starting at
or after checkout time. This handles cases where the
checked-out event has already been filtered out.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
Feat(checkin): Implement core state machine

Add four-state occupancy tracking state machine to
CheckinTrackingSensor with timer-based transitions,
HA event bus firing, post-checkout linger timing for
all FR-006 scenarios, FR-007 identity protection, and
FR-030 auto check-out rescheduling. Add 21 unit tests
covering state transitions, event identity, event bus
payloads, cancellation handling, and timer management.
